content_en:
Muhammad Zahid ibn al-Hasan al-Kawthari (1296–1371 AH / 1879–1951 CE) was among the most formidable hadith and fiqh scholars of the twentieth century, a product of the Ottoman scholarly tradition who served as deputy to the last Shaykh al-Islam of the Ottoman Empire before the abolition of the caliphate in 1924. A Hanafi jurist and Maturidi theologian, al-Kawthari was known equally for the breadth of his learning and for the sharpness of his polemical pen — qualities that were inseparable in his scholarly character. After the collapse of the Ottoman state he lived in exile in Egypt and Syria, continuing to write prolifically on hadith criticism, theology, and questions of legal history until his death in Cairo. His footnotes to classical works and his independent treatises together form a substantial and still-studied corpus.

Al-Nasihah al-Kafiyah liman Yatawalla Yazid — roughly translated as "The Sufficient Counsel for Whoever Takes Yazid as a Patron" — addresses one of the most sensitive historical and theological questions in Sunni Islam: the moral and religious status of Yazid ibn Mu'awiyah, the second Umayyad caliph under whose reign the tragedy of Karbala occurred in 61 AH. The question of whether it is permissible to curse Yazid by name, to consider him a fasiq, or to take a position of silence has divided Sunni scholars across the centuries. Al-Kawthari's treatise defends the mainstream Ash'ari-Hanafi scholarly position that neither blanket approval nor formal cursing of Yazid is warranted, while engaging in detail with the historical narrations and the arguments of those who take contrary views in either direction.

The methodology of the work is characteristic of al-Kawthari's broader scholarly approach: close engagement with primary texts, rigorous examination of the chains of narrations used to build arguments about Yazid's character and conduct, and careful attention to the principles of theology (kalam) that govern how Muslims should speak of historical figures associated with grave events. Al-Kawthari draws on classical Hanafi and Ash'ari authorities, critiques narrations he considers weak or misapplied, and situates the question within the broader framework of how Ahl al-Sunnah navigate historical controversies without falling into either Kharijite denunciation or Umayyad partisanship. The treatise is not a defense of Yazid's conduct but a defense of scholarly restraint and methodological discipline.

Readers should approach Al-Nasihah al-Kafiyah with awareness of its context and limits. It represents one respected scholarly position within Ahl al-Sunnah wal-Jama'ah — the Ash'ari-Hanafi mainstream that dominated Ottoman and later Egyptian scholarly institutions — and should be read alongside the range of Sunni scholarly views on this question. It is not a work of Shia theology, nor does it promote any sectarian agenda; al-Kawthari was a committed Sunni scholar who elsewhere wrote extensively against Shia and other heterodox positions. The treatise is valuable for students of Islamic history, kalam, and hadith criticism who wish to understand how major Sunni scholars have reasoned carefully about contested historical questions without departing from the methodology of Ahl al-Sunnah.
content_id:
Muhammad Zahid ibn al-Hasan al-Kawthari (1296-1371 H / 1879-1951 M) adalah salah satu ulama hadis dan fikih paling terkemuka pada abad kedua puluh, seorang produk tradisi keilmuan Ottoman yang pernah menjabat sebagai wakil Syaikh al-Islam terakhir Kekaisaran Ottoman sebelum penghapusan kekhalifahan pada tahun 1924. Sebagai ahli fikih Hanafi dan teolog Maturidi, al-Kawthari dikenal karena keluasan ilmunya sekaligus ketajaman penanya dalam perdebatan keilmuan. Setelah runtuhnya negara Ottoman, beliau hidup di pengasingan di Mesir dan Suriah, terus menulis secara produktif tentang kritik hadis, teologi, dan pertanyaan-pertanyaan sejarah hukum hingga wafatnya di Kairo.

Al-Nasihah al-Kafiyah liman Yatawalla Yazid yang berarti Nasihat yang Cukup bagi Siapa yang Menjadikan Yazid sebagai Pelindung membahas salah satu persoalan sejarah dan teologi paling sensitif dalam Islam Sunni, yaitu status moral dan keagamaan Yazid ibn Mu`awiyah, khalifah Umayyah kedua yang di bawah pemerintahannya terjadi tragedi Karbala pada tahun 61 H. Al-Kawthari membela posisi arus utama Ash`ari-Hanafi yang menyatakan bahwa tidak ada alasan untuk memberikan persetujuan penuh maupun untuk melaknat Yazid secara resmi.

Metode karya ini mencerminkan pendekatan keilmuan khas al-Kawthari: keterlibatan mendalam dengan teks-teks primer, pemeriksaan ketat terhadap sanad riwayat yang digunakan untuk membangun argumen tentang karakter Yazid, dan perhatian cermat terhadap prinsip-prinsip aqidah yang mengatur bagaimana kaum Muslim seharusnya berbicara tentang tokoh-tokoh sejarah yang terkait dengan peristiwa-peristiwa besar. Al-Kawthari menyandarkan karyanya pada otoritas-otoritas klasik Hanafi dan Ash`ari, mengkritisi riwayat-riwayat yang ia anggap lemah, dan menempatkan persoalan ini dalam kerangka bagaimana Ahl al-Sunnah menavigasi kontroversi sejarah dengan disiplin metodologi.

Para pembaca hendaknya mendekati Al-Nasihah al-Kafiyah dengan memahami konteks dan batasannya. Karya ini mewakili satu posisi keilmuan yang dihormati dalam Ahl al-Sunnah wal-Jama`ah yaitu arus utama Ash`ari-Hanafi yang mendominasi institusi keilmuan Ottoman dan kemudian Mesir. Karya ini berharga bagi para pelajar sejarah Islam, ilmu kalam, dan kritik hadis yang ingin memahami bagaimana ulama-ulama Sunni terkemuka telah menalar secara cermat tentang persoalan-persoalan sejarah yang diperdebatkan.
